data.description: Morgan Stanley now sees the Bank of England holding rates steady through 2026, shifting away from earlier expectations of rate cuts in April, November, and February 2027.

data.title: FedEx, Planet Labs And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Friday
data.description: This morning, U.S. stock futures are trading higher, potentially drawing investor attention to specific stocks today. Xpeng Inc. (NYSE: XPEV) is expected to announce its fourth-quarter earnings before the market opens, with shares rising 0.4% to $19.23 in after-hours trading. Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL) reported stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter sales and provided FY27 guidance surpassing estimates, leading to a 14.6% surge in shares to $30.90 during after-hours trading. FedEx Corp. also remains in focus. Full details are available on a financial news website.

data.title: Why Torrid Holdings (CURV) Stock Surged 20% After Hours
data.description: Shares of Torrid Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CURV) surged by 20% in after-hours trading on Thursday, reaching $1.50. The California-based retailer specializing in plus-size clothing released its fourth-quarter and fiscal 2025 results for the period ending Jan. 31. The full-year adjusted EBITDA for Torrid Holdings surpassed expectations, coming in at $63.6 million, with net sales reaching $1.0 billion. However, the company reported a net loss of $7 million compared to a net income of $16.3 million the previous year. Total liquidity was noted at $84.9 million. CEO Lisa Harper described 2025 as a "transformational year" and indicated positive first-quarter trends. In the fourth quarter, net sales declined by 14.3% to $236.2 million.

data.title: Arcfra AECP 6.3 Breaks the 11M IOPS Barrier, Delivering Tier-1 All-Flash Performance and RPO=0 Resilience for Enterprise Cloud
data.description: Today, in Singapore on March 20, 2026, Arcfra, an innovator in high-performance cloud and AI technology, unveiled the launch of Arcfra Enterprise Cloud Platform (AECP) 6.3. Fueled by the cutting-edge Arcfra Cloud Operating System (ACOS 6.3), this upgrade brings a significant increase of 4.6 times in performance compared to the previous version.

data.title: Decoding TOPCon: A JA Solar-University of New South Wales Dialogue on the Future of PV
data.description: In March 20, 2026, the global photovoltaic (PV) industry is facing challenges such as cost constraints, policy changes, and rapid technological advancements. TOPCon technology has become integral in current solar manufacturing practices, representing the majority of new module production on a global scale. This technology is instrumental in driving efficiency and innovation within the industry, with a significant impact on deployment worldwide.

data.title: Galderma Publishes Invitation and Agenda for Its 2026 Annual General Meeting
data.description: Galderma Group AG, a prominent player in dermatology (SIX: GALD), announced details for its upcoming 2026 Annual General Meeting, scheduled for April 22, 2026, at 3:00pm CEST, to be held virtually. The Board of Directors recommends a dividend of 0.35 CHF per Galderma share, reflecting the company's strong performance in 2025. This dividend proposal will be sourced from capital contribution reserves pending shareholder approval.

data.title: Telix selects IBA Cyclone® KIUBE to support manufacturing expansion in the U.S.
data.description: On March 20, 2026, IBA, a global leader in particle accelerator technology and radiopharmaceutical production solutions, revealed a new contract with Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited. The agreement entails Telix purchasing four Cyclone ® KIUBE cyclotrons from IBA at standard conditions 1 for installation at specific RLS Radiopharmacies (RLS) sites in the U.S. Telix, a precision oncology company, is listed on the ASX (TLX) and NASDAQ (TLX). This collaboration marks a significant milestone for both companies in the field of radiopharmaceutical production.

data.title: Novartis agrees to acquire a pan-mutant-selective PI3Kα inhibitor, strengthening its breast cancer pipeline
data.description: In Basel on March 20, 2026, Novartis revealed a new deal to acquire SNV4818 from Synnovation Therapeutics, LLC. SNV4818 is a pan-mutant-selective PI3Kα inhibitor that offers a potential advanced remedy for patients with HR+/HER2- breast cancer and other solid tumor cases. This move signifies Novartis' commitment to exploring innovative treatment options and signifies their dedication to improving cancer care with cutting-edge therapies.

data.title: AI agents like Claude can now create USDC cross-chain apps using Circle's toolkit, enhancing stability and reducing developer uncertainty. (Read More)
data.description: Circle has introduced an open-source toolkit that assists AI assistants like Claude in developing stablecoin applications following proper implementation patterns, which alleviates developer uncertainty. This toolkit aims to streamline the process and enhance the stability of these apps, potentially impacting a considerable number of developers working on similar projects.

data.title: TGS 2025 Annual Report
data.description: In Oslo, Norway, TGS, a prominent global supplier of energy data and analysis, released its 2025 Annual Report on 20th March 2026.

data.title: VALLOUREC TO SHOWCASE THE LATEST INNOVATIONS  IN ITS VAM® CONNECTIONS AND COMPREHENSIVE  SERVICE OFFERINGS AT OTC ASIA 2026
data.description: Vallourec will present its newest innovations in VAM® connections and full-service solutions at OTC Asia 2026. This exhibition will feature state-of-the-art products and services for the oil and gas industry, highlighting advancements in connection technology and comprehensive offerings. Attendees can expect to see cutting-edge solutions in Vallourec's VAM® line of products, demonstrating the company's commitment to excellence and innovation. Visit the Vallourec booth at OTC Asia 2026 to learn more about their latest developments and contributions to the energy sector.
Barclays now forecasts that the European Central Bank will raise rates by 25 bp each in April and June 2026, shifting from a previous hold outlook.

data.title: Super Micro co-founder detained for purported $2.5B AI chip trafficking plot; accused of channeling servers worth $2.5 billion to China.
data.description: American officials have announced the arrest and charging of Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, a co-founder of Supermicro. The charges allege that Liaw facilitated the transfer of $2.5 billion worth of AI servers to China by using shell companies.

data.title: BTC approaches 10% decline amid concerns over support reliability, with recent correction from highs.
data.description: Bitcoin recently experienced a correction of approximately 10% from its recent local highs, leading to increased doubts regarding the long-term price support for BTC.
